title: ADR-0011: Tenant-to-cell registry — the platform's only global component
verdict_summary: Canonical ratified ADR (2026-07-07). Decision record that ratifies FR-I19 / A-CELL-REGISTRY. Its core build is comprehensively covered by identity Epic E6 (Global org->cell registry & routing): two schema-isolated stores (routing core + sensitive shard, E6-S1), cell assignment at creation + global hostname uniqueness (E6-S2), Workers KV mirror + edge geo-router read path / no-SPOF (E6-S3), tenant-move step-API (E6-S4), cell-guard/421 middleware (E8-S2), and the cell-claim PARSE side in lib pkg/auth (E1-S1, TestCellClaimParse, AC3 covers short-TTL session tokens carry cell/cell_epoch while long-lived bearer/MCP do not). FR-I19 is in covers-union. Legal PII-free classification is an acknowledged OPEN external gate (escalate at M14), not a build blocker. One thin BUILD gap: the mint/write side of decision #4 (carrier-not-owner cell claim) — mirroring cell into Clerk org/user metadata + a Keycloak mapper so minted session tokens actually CARRY cell + cell_epoch — has no explicit story; the parse-side story merely assumes the claim is present.

gaps.quote: identity mirrors the cell into Clerk org/user metadata + a Keycloak mapper so every minted session token carries `cell` + `cell_epoch`. `cell` / `cell_epoch` live ONLY in short-TTL session/access tokens; long-lived bearer + scoped MCP tokens carry no cell claim (routed by the stable tenant claim via KV).
gaps.why_uncovered: The cell-claim PARSE/verify side is covered (lib E1-S1 AC3 parses cell/cell_epoch and asserts long-lived tokens legitimately lack it), but that story ASSUMES the token already carries the claim. No story builds the write path: mirroring the assigned cell + cell_epoch into Clerk org/user metadata and configuring the Keycloak mapper so short-TTL session/access tokens are minted WITH cell/cell_epoch (and bearer/MCP tokens without). Grep across all breakdown plans + bodies + feature-map + covers-union found no story mirroring cell into Clerk metadata or a Keycloak mapper; E6-S2 (cell assignment) writes the registry row but does not mention the IdP metadata mirror.
gaps.suggested_home: identity Epic E6 (Global org->cell registry & routing) as a new story wiring the cell/cell_epoch mirror into Clerk org metadata + Keycloak mapper at assignment/tenant-move, OR fold into identity Epic E4 (exchange/session mint) so the minted engine session token injects the current cell pin + epoch.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED: source is live canon (docmost-cli confirms Status: Canonical, ratified 2026-07-07, not superseded). Grepped feature-map.txt, all breakdown/*/plan.json covers[], and every identity/lib body (E6-S1..S4 store+assign+KV+move, E4-S1/S4 exchange+Keycloak-OIDC-relocation, E1-S1 parse-side) plus a repo-wide grep for publicMetadata/privateMetadata/Clerk-metadata/Keycloak-mapper -- zero stories touch minting cell/cell_epoch into Clerk org/user metadata or a Keycloak mapper. ENG-2689/2690 is the unrelated browser-extension pack; ENG-2101/2092 are pack-certification dispatch gates on existing stories, not a story that owns this write path. This is genuine build work (application + IdP-config-as-code), not process/ops -- gap survives.

components.name: tenant->cell routing registry (dedicated global CNPG, identity sole writer)
components.quote: the registry lives in a dedicated global CNPG owned by identity, logically outside every cell

components.name: PII-free routing core store (tenant_id, cell_id, residency_pin, cell_epoch, status)
components.quote: PII-free routing core (`tenant_id, cell_id, residency_pin, cell_epoch, status`) is cross-jurisdiction-replicable and mirrored to Workers KV

components.name: in-jurisdiction sensitive shard (stripe_customer, hostname_claims, contacts)
components.quote: an in-jurisdiction sensitive shard (`stripe_customer`, hostname\_claims, contacts)

components.name: Workers KV mirror of the routing core
components.quote: replicable and mirrored to Workers KV

components.name: account-based discovery endpoint (discover-once-then-pin) + 421 Misdirected Request semantics
components.quote: An account-based discovery endpoint returns the tenant's cell host; clients discover-once-then-pin; a stale cell host returns 421 Misdirected Request -> exactly one re-discovery + retry

components.name: Clerk org/user metadata cell mirror + Keycloak cell mapper
components.quote: identity mirrors the cell into Clerk org/user metadata + a Keycloak mapper so every minted session token carries `cell` + `cell_epoch`
